headline: Iran war shows norms of international conflicts have been upended
summary: During the war missiles have also landed close to nuclear facilities in Iran and Israel. The World Health Organisation said that the conflict was at a 'perilous stage' and called for restraint.

headline: Oil above $100 over conflicting claims on US-Iran talks
summary: The US repeated claims it is in discussions with Iran while Shell warned oil shortages will hit Europe.

headline: Russia launches 948 drones at Ukraine in largest attack over a 24-hour period
summary: Cities across Ukraine were hit, with local officials reporting at least eight people killed and dozens injured.

headline: Trump cites progress with Iran, US proposes plan to end war
summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday the U.S. was making progress in its efforts to negotiate an end to war with Iran, including winning an important concession from Tehran, while a source confirmed that Washington had sent Iran a proposal.

headline: SK Hynix files for US listing that source says could raise up to $14 billion
summary: South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix has filed for a U.S. listing that could raise up to $14 billion, according to sources.

headline: Trump administration offers 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran
summary: At least 1,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division will be sent to the Mideast in the coming days. The Trump administration has offered a 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran according to a person briefed on the contours of the proposal.

headline: US sends Iran a ceasefire plan but Tehran dismisses talks
summary: Iran's attacks on regional energy infrastructure along with its restrictions on the strait have sent oil prices skyrocketing and sparked fears of a global energy crisis. At least 1,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division will be sent to the Mideast.

headline: Iran receives US ceasefire proposal, Pakistan officials say
summary: Khalid al-Yaqoubi, security advisor to Iraq's prime minister, says his country will file a sovereignty complaint to the United Nations Security Council. Iraqi security forces have lost 80 members and 150 have been wounded since the Middle East war began.
